zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Fiennes Briennes於大約1273年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾文多福時，她的父親Guillaume de Fiennes II35歲，母親Blanche de Brienne28歲。她和Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於大約1309年10月26日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯享年37歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Fiennes Briennes于大约1273年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾文多福时，她的父亲Guillaume de Fiennes II35岁，母亲Blanche de Brienne28岁。她和Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于大约1309年10月26日在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Fiennes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Finnis
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: locative name from Fiennes (Pas-de-Calais).  See also  Filtness  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Fiennes Briennes was born about 1273, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Guillaume de Fiennes II, was 35 and her mother, Blanche de Brienne, was 28. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell. She died about 26 October 1309, in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 37.

Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell. Elle est décédée environ 26 octobre 1309, à Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 37 ans.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Fiennes Briennes nasceu aproximadamente 1273, em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Guillaume de Fiennes II, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Blanche de Brienne, tinha 28 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 26 de outubro de 1309, em Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Fiennes Briennes родилась около 1273 г. в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Guillaume de Fiennes II было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Blanche de Brienne, 28. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Baron John Wake Ist Baron of Liddell. Она умерла около 26 октября 1309 г. в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 37 лет.
